architecture.summary: A retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system that integrates large language models with knowledge graphs for fault diagnosis in new energy vehicles. The system operates in three stages: (1) fault data collection from NEV sources, (2) structured knowledge graph construction via ChatGLM3-6B performing named entity and relation extraction over 122 fault categories, and (3) fault knowledge model training using domain-optimized fine-tuned BERT for fault classification. Query processing routes through the knowledge graph for retrieval, augments the LLM context with retrieved fault knowledge, and generates multi-level diagnostic responses with cause analysis and solution recommendations.
trained_components: BERT model fine-tuned for NEV fault classification
knowledge graph construction via ChatGLM3-6B entity/relation extraction

biblio.title: Large Language Model Based Intelligent Fault Information Retrieval System for New Energy Vehicles

claims.statement: A domain-optimized fine-tuning strategy for LLMs based on NEV fault characteristics, verifying the superior accuracy of the Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) model in fault classification tasks

A structured knowledge graph encompassing 122 fault categories, developed through the ChatGLM3-6B model completing named entity and knowledge relation extraction to generate fault knowledge and build a paraphrased vocabulary

An intelligent fault information retrieval system that significantly outperforms traditional models in NEV-specific Q&A scenarios, providing multi-level fault cause analysis and actionable solution recommendations
